name: recall
description: Persistent semantic memory system. Stores, searches and connects memories across a categorized knowledge base. Uses a bidirectional typed graph for rich semantic relationships between memories. Expands on the static USER/SOUL core files with dynamic, evolving personal knowledge.

category.description: Top-level cognitive model. Self_Model (internal evolution, growth, calibrations), User_Model (user profile, projects, social, routines, psychology), Semantic_Memory (external knowledge, places, facts).

subcategory.description: Subcategory path (can be nested with '/'). E.g. 'People/Family/Siblings', 'Lifestyle/Health/Mental_Wellbeing', 'Beliefs/Opinions/Technology'.

data.description: Memory content. Use rich Markdown. For 'synapse' (create) and 'refresh' (update).

query.description: Search string or memory ID. Used by: recall, refresh, amnesia.

tags.description: Semantic tags. Critical for cross-category discovery. Use specific, meaningful terms. E.g. ['anxiety', 'music', 'coping'].

linked_to.description: Explicit semantic links to existing memories. Creates typed bidirectional graph edges. Use during 'synapse'.

relation.description: Edge type describing the semantic relationship.

from_id.description: Source memory ID for the 'link' command.

to_id.description: Target memory ID for the 'link' command.

depth.description: Graph traversal depth for 'recall'. 1 = direct neighbors only, 2 = two hops (default), 3 = broad exploration.
